title: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE: India, Netherlands elevating ties to strategic partnership, says Modi
content: PM Narendra Modi on May 16 met King of the Netherlands Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima at the Royal Palace and discussed close cooperation in digital technology, innovation, fintech and blue economy

title: S.P. Velumani | From loyalist to rebel
content: Once seen as Edappadi Palaniswamy’s trusted lieutenant, the AIADMK legislator and former Minister is now leading a revolt within the party following its electoral defeat

title: Centre to roll out pilot programme for linking out-of-school adolescents to open school
content: The initiative will include app-based mapping of out of school children in the age group of 14-18 years, monitoring systems, incentive mechanisms, and district-level convergence strategies

title: Karjat MLA Rohit Pawar hits out at CJI comment on youngsters
content: Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi pulled up a lawyer for “pursuing” a senior advocate designation, saying that there are already “parasites” in society who attack the system, and asked the petitioner whether he wanted to join hands with them

title: BNI meet begins in Jaipur with focus on long-term growth opportunities for startups
content: The two-day conference of the BNI, considered as the world’s largest business networking organisation, seeks to create a platform for building trusted business relationships and fostering new opportunities

title: TMC sends fact-finding teams across Bengal, alleges attacks and killings after poll defeat
content: Party claims over 3,000 workers were attacked and at least 10 killed in post-poll violence; BJP dismisses exercise as “drama” while Calcutta High Court orders safe return of victims

title: India stands at a historic juncture where it possesses a confluence youth power, diversity, scientific temperament, democratic spirit: Karnataka Governor
content: We must ensure that innovation is accessible to every individual in our society. When technology is dedicated to the service of humanity, it truly becomes a catalyst for nation-building

title: India will need 1.5 lakh company secretaries by 2047: ICSI President
content: As of today, the requirement is 10,000 company secretaries, President of The Institute of Company Secretaries of India said at the sidelines of Tamil Nadu State Conference

title: U.P. CM Adityanath lays foundation stone of international cricket stadium in Gorakhpur
content: Speaking about the Tal Nador area, Yogi Adityanath said it was barren and neglected until a few years ago, and that encroachments had since been cleared

title: AIADMK’s rebel camp in no mood to seek early convening of general council
content: A key leader from the camp says: “For the time being, all our focus is on getting the issue of the legislature party resolved at the earliest.”

title: Guntur civic chief orders strict vigil against contamination of drinking water
content: Officials told to conduct special audit of drinking water pipelines near the drainage channels and ensure proper documentation of quality test results

title: PM SHRI set to be a tightrope walk for UDF government in Kerala
content: Nearly ₹1,100 crore of Samagra Shiksha funds have been withheld by the Union government since 2023-24; CM-designate V.D. Satheesan had slammed the LDF govt. when it signed on to the scheme in October last year

title: India's aspirations ‘no longer limited to its borders’: Modi to diaspora in Netherlands
content: The Prime Minister, in the second leg of his five-nation tour, meets King and Queen of the Netherlands, addresses Indian diaspora in the European nation

title: Kuki-Zo protestors rally in Manipur, demanding justice for slain church leaders, release of hostages
content: Kuki-Zo groups call for re-imposition of President’s Rule in memo to Home Minister; claiming 14 people are still hostages, Kuki Inpi Manipur chief says “continued captivity is an act of terror against a people”

title: Plan to turn Chinnaganjam beaches into tourism hubs, says Bapatla District Collector
content: Vinod Kumar says says beaches in the mandal will be modelled on Suryalanka and Chirala to boost tourism, revenue generation and local livelihoods

title: GGHs in A.P. told to submit reports on biomedical waste management
content: The instructions come after a dog fled with an amputated limb from the Government General Hospital premises in Vijayawada

title: Probe ordered after dogs flee with amputated limb from government hospital in Vijayawada
content: NTR district Collector Lakshmisha has ordered inquiry into the incident, and directed the GGH authorities to take action against the negligent staff

title: Chola-era Anaimangalam Plates, in possession of Leiden University since 1862, returned to India
content: The plates will be handed over to the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in New Delhi; the ASI, which functions under the Union Ministry of Culture, will decide whether and where the objects will be exhibited in India.

title: Over 1.76 lakh students register for TNEA 2026
content: Over 1.02 lakh among them have completed payment, while 62,863 of them have uploaded their certificates on the portal

title: Solar-powered blinkers installed at 51 identified hotspots in Ranipet to prevent accidents
content: Narrowness of the carriageway, curves with poor visibility, lack of road markings, pothole-ridden stretches, and a lack of better streetlights are found to be reasons for fatal accidents

title: Village-wise crop planning needed for sustainable farming: Pemmasani
content: Union Minister calls for seed villages, crop diversification and technology-driven awareness programmes for farmers in Guntur district

title: V.D. Satheesan | The ‘Nehruvian’ revivalist
content: A self-described supporter of Nehruvian socialism, the Congress leader who steered the party-led alliance, out of power for a decade, to an emphatic victory over the Left Democratic Front in Kerala, is now set to take the reins of the State

title: Full list of Kerala Cabinet Ministers by May 17, says Satheesan
content: Full Council of Ministers to be sworn in on May 18 morning, says CM-designate; rumours suggest he may hold on to the Finance portfolio while allotting Home to his vanquished rival Ramesh Chennithala

title: NEET-UG | Test in turmoil
content: Following a question-paper leak, this year’s medical entrance exam, taken by over 22 lakh students, has been cancelled and rescheduled

title: Gravity of a crime must not dictate decision on premature release of a convict: Supreme Court
content: Denial of remission cannot rest solely on the ground of the heinousness of the crime, says SC; instead, the executive must consider prisoner’s conduct, evidence of reformation, and prospects of reintegration

title: Handed over my son for police inquiry through lawyers with due respect for law: Bandi Sanjay
content: The lawyers, after examining the available evidence, said that the case will be quashed and bail will be granted. As a result, a slight delay occurred, Mr. Sanjay said

title: NEET paper leak row: Congress demands resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Mallikarjun Kharge alleged that more than 90 examination papers had been leaked during the Modi government’s tenure, adversely affecting over 90 million students and their families

title: Youth are pillars of country, remarks aimed at those with ‘fake’ degrees: CJI Surya Kant
content: “What I had specifically criticised were those who have entered professions like the Bar with the aid of fake and bogus degrees”, the Chief Justice said

title: Athlete Swapna Barman becomes victim of poll violence, house attacked
content: Trinamool Congress sent fact-finding teams to different districts of the State to ascertain the extent of post-poll violence

title: Average of 53% MPs attend meetings of 16 Parliamentary Standing Committees, PAC: data
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has been raising the issue of ensuring that MPs attend parliamentary committees; since reconstituting the panels in September 2025, five meetings were postponed due to a lack of quorum

title: Mayawati demands extensive relief for storm victims in Uttar Pradesh
content: The BSP president also criticised the Centre for increasing petrol and diesel prices by three rupees per litre, emphasising its detrimental effect on poor and middle-class families

title: Hindu groups perform puja at Bhojshala amid security; ASI issues directions to implement High Court order
content: While Hindu groups termed the High Court verdict “historic and the result of a 700-year struggle”, Muslim representatives say it is “one-sided”

title: Operation Epic Fury to resume? Trump weighs fresh Iran strikes after China visit: Report
content: As US President Trump returns from China, pressure mounts over Iran. Top aides are reportedly preparing options for renewed military action against Tehran if diplomacy falters, including intensified bombing and potential Special Operations deployments.  Iran, meanwhile, signals readiness for conflict, while the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical concern for global oil prices.

title: Kolkata survive Gill scare: KKR crack GT code to keep playoff hopes breathing
content: Chasing 248, Gujarat Titans finished on 218 for 4 after Kolkata’s bowlers held their nerve in the death overs.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Chennithala tipped for key role as Satheesan finalises ministry list
content: Kerala's new Congress government is set to take shape. CM-designate V D Satheesan will present the cabinet list to the governor on Sunday. Ramesh Chennithala is expected to secure key portfolios. Several other leaders are also likely to be included. Final decisions on portfolios will be made after discussions with allies.

title: 'Stop spending billions on subsidies; establish a plan': Musk's Spacex in letter to FCC
content: SpaceX is urging the FCC to end a $4.5 billion fund that subsidizes rural broadband. The satellite internet company argues its services have already solved the connectivity gap by offering fast, competitive rates nationwide. SpaceX believes these legacy programs are now redundant due to technological advancements and increasing competition.

title: ‘Will erode goodwill’: Congress warns Vijay against inducting rebel AIADMK MLAs into Tamil Nadu govt
content: Congress has cautioned Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay against incorporating rebel AIADMK members into his government.  Karti Chidambaram stressed that the public mandate was for a government independent of both AIADMK and DMK.  Inducting the splinter group, he warned, would significantly damage Vijay's goodwill and credibility, urging him to remain true to the electoral mandate.

title: After Microsoft gave engineers access to Anthropic's Claude Code, company is canceling licenses
content: Microsoft is phasing out internal licenses for Anthropic's Claude Code AI tool, shifting developers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI platform. This strategic move, effective by the end of June, aims to streamline AI coding tools and align with Microsoft's engineering needs and financial year-end.

title: Maharashtra turns Maoist-free, last rebels taken out in 100-hour operation
content: Maharashtra has declared Gadchiroli district Maoist-free following a 100-hour operation that resulted in zero active Maoists in police records. The coordinated action, codenamed 'Antim Prahar,' led to eight arrests and five surrenders, dismantling the Maoists' military wing and crippling their financial resources.

title: Stephen Curry’s mom Sonya Curry shares powerful advice for parents
content: Sonya Curry recently shared heartfelt parenting advice while reflecting on motherhood, self-identity and the importance of intentional living. Speaking with Today’s Jenna & Sheinelle, the mother of Stephen and Seth Curry encouraged parents to stay connected to their own passions while raising children. Her comments also echoed earlier reflections on balancing family responsibilities during Dell Curry’s NBA career. Sonya’s honesty about the challenges of parenting and managing demanding schedules gave fans a deeper look into the family dynamic that helped shape two successful NBA careers.

title: Theo Von reacts after Joe Rogan's emotional breakdown comments go viral
content: Theo Von pushed back against Joe Rogan after the UFC commentator questioned his emotional health and antidepressant use during recent discussions. Von said he is doing fine and criticized the online narrative around him. The situation follows a tense earlier podcast appearance that sparked debate online. Rogan also recently drew attention for comments questioning the timing of government UFO file releases.

title: Leon Draisaitl and wife Celeste Desjardins' net worth, salary and earnings
content: In 2026, the combined wealth of Edmonton Oilers star Leon Draisaitl and Canadian actress Celeste Desjardins grows. Draisaitl’s large NHL contracts, endorsement deals and career earnings continue to be the biggest contributors, while Desjardins has grown her entertainment and modeling success. Their improving financial profile has been a hot topic in NHL news roundup conversations this year.

title: Italy: 8 injured after driver crashes car into pedestrians in Modena
content: A car deliberately drove into pedestrians in Modena, Italy, injuring at least eight people, with four seriously hurt. The driver, an Italian national of North African origin, was arrested after brandishing a knife. Prime Minister Meloni called the incident "extremely serious" and thanked those who intervened.

title: Anti-terror search operation launched across J&K’s Poonch district
content: Jammu and Kashmir's Poonch district is under a massive security sweep following a foiled infiltration bid. Forces are conducting extensive searches across multiple locations after an intruder was neutralized near the Line of Control. Heightened security measures, including stringent vehicle checks, are in place amid unconfirmed reports of a high-profile foreign terrorist presence.

title: 'Didn’t deserve to win': Gill’s brutal verdict after GT defeat to KKR
content: Shubman Gill attributed Gujarat Titans' 29-run loss to Kolkata Knight Riders to poor fielding, stating the team "didn't deserve to win" after dropping multiple crucial catches. Despite a strong batting performance led by Gill's 85, GT fell short of KKR's 248, with dropped catches allowing KKR's batsmen to score heavily.

title: After China, US unveils ultra long range air-to-air missile — where does India stand
content: India is bolstering its air-to-air missile capabilities with indigenous Astra Mk-2 and the upcoming Astra Mk-3 Gandiva, boasting ranges of 200 km and 350 km respectively. This strategic move aims to counter threats and achieve parity with advanced US and Chinese weaponry, reducing reliance on foreign suppliers and ensuring national security in ultra-long-range aerial combat.

title: Pathirana leaves field mid-over on debut; KKR’s 18 crore gamble under threat
content: Matheesha Pathirana's much-anticipated IPL 2026 debut for KKR ended prematurely due to an injury scare, just eight balls into his spell.  Despite the concern, the Sri Lankan pacer completed his over before leaving the field. This happened as KKR's batters, led by Finn Allen's explosive 93, dominated Gujarat Titans, posting a massive 247/2.

title: 'Illegally constituted': MEA rejects Pakistan-backed court ruling on Indus Treaty
content: India has once again rejected the Court of Arbitration formed under the Indus Waters Treaty. The External Affairs Ministry stated that India never recognized the court's establishment. Any proceedings or awards from this court are considered null and void. India's decision to keep the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance remains unchanged.

title: 'Darling, leaked papers for you': What Lucknow teacher told female student
content: An assistant professor from Lucknow University is accused of leaking examination papers and making inappropriate advances towards a female student. Viral audio clips allegedly capture the professor promising academic favors and leaked papers in exchange for meetings. The university has filed a complaint, leading to the professor's detention and an ongoing investigation.

title: As Ukraine takes the lead in robot in combat, India deploys them on LoC
content: Ukraine is at the forefront of a military upheaval, unleashing a fleet of Unmanned Ground Vehicles, such as cutting-edge combat cyborgs and self-destructing attack machines, which are key to keeping troops safe on the front lines. By assigning perilous missions to these tenacious robots, soldiers can concentrate on more aggressive tactics.

title: NEET paper leak row: Delhi court sends two accused to 10-day CBI custody
content: The CBI has secured 10 days custody of accused Manisha Waghmare and PV Kulkarni in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case. Both are alleged to be part of a larger conspiracy to obtain and distribute leaked exam papers. Investigators claim a senior teacher leaked Biology questions, while a retired professor was involved in the question paper setting panel.

title: Splitsvilla X6 - Pyaar Ya Paisa winners: Gullu aka Kushal Tanwar and Kaira Anu lift the trophy
content: The grand finale of Splitsvilla X6, themed 'Pyaar Ya Paisa', saw Kushal Tanwar, affectionately called Gullu, and Kaira Anu take home the championship title. Their bond and impressive gameplay kept viewers at the edge of their seats, ultimately leading to a triumph that was celebrated by many.

title: Sunil Narine scripts history, becomes first overseas player to...
content: Sunil Narine achieved a significant milestone on Saturday, completing 200 Indian Premier League appearances. He is the first overseas player to reach this mark. Narine has been a key player for Kolkata Knight Riders since 2012. He is recognized for his exceptional bowling economy and has also become a powerful hitter.

title: Ex-merchant navy captain ‘sold’ youths from UP to cyber slavery hubs in Cambodia; held
content: A former merchant navy captain, Nagesh Kumar, has been apprehended for allegedly orchestrating a cyber slavery ring. He is accused of luring Indian youths with false job promises to Cambodia, where they were forced into fraudulent call centers. The racket, exposed by victims, involved trafficking individuals to Southeast Asia and exploiting them for financial gain.

title: 'No feud with KC Venugopal': Kerala CM-designate VD Satheesan breaks silence
content: Kerala CM-designate VD Satheesan and senior leader KC Venugopal dismissed reports of internal feuds, asserting that all organizational and cabinet decisions are made jointly through consultations. The oath-taking ceremony for the new Congress-led UDF government is scheduled for May 18, with cabinet formation discussions underway.

title: Centre approves key NTA appointments as uproar over NEET paper leak intensifies
content: Amidst the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak scandal, the government has appointed four new officials to key positions within the National Testing Agency (NTA). The CBI continues its investigation, recently arresting a senior teacher from Pune identified as a key source of leaked Biology questions. Protests and legal challenges are escalating, with demands for accountability and the NTA's dissolution.

title: Netherlands Returns 1,000-Year-Old Chola-Era Copper Plates To India
content: The artefacts, widely referred to in Europe as the 'Leiden Plates', are among the most historically valuable surviving records from the Chola empire. India had been seeking their return since 2012.

title: Rescue Diver Dies In Search Of 4 Italians Who Drowned In Maldives
content: Mohamed Mahudhee, a member of the Maldivian National Defense Force, died of underwater decompression sickness.

title: Average Students, Extraordinary NEET Scores: Curious Case Of The Rajasthan 5
content: Five children from the Biwal family managed to clear NEET at the same time last year. Three members of the family are key accused in the NEET leak.

title: NDTV Exclusive: Inside British Museum Housing Bhojshala's Saraswati Idol
content: In its judgement declaring the Bhojshala complex a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati or Vagdevi, the court also asked the government to consider formal steps for bringing back the historic idol currently housed in the British Museum in London.

title: Amid Centre's 'Save Fuel' Push, Nostalgic Comeback For Tongas In Jammu Town
content: The Tonga tradition had indeed gone out of fashion, but the fuel conservation drive has brought it back to life.

title: Rs 42 Lakh Compensation To Graphic Designer Injured In 2021 Crash In Maharashtra
content: The Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) in Maharashtra's Thane district has awarded a compensation of Rs 41.81 lakh to a 28-year-old graphic designer who suffered 60 per cent permanent disability following a road accident in 2021.

title: Centre Appoints 2 Joint Secretaries, Joint Directors In National Testing Agency
content: The Centre on Saturday appointed two each joint secretaries and joint directors in the National Testing Agency (NTA), which is under the heat over the NEET-UG paper leak.

title: Ex-ISRO Scientist's Rs 1,500 Crore Startup Eyes Maiden Orbital Rocket Launch
content: In eight years after leaving his secure job at ISRO, where he earned Rs 75,000 a month, Chandana, along with his cofounder Naga Bharath Daka, has created a company now valued at Rs 1,500 crore.

title: Tata Electronics Teams Up With ASML For Semiconductor Manufacturing In India
content: Electronics manufacturing company Tata Electronics and Dutch semiconductor equipment major ASML have joined hands to enable chip manufacturing in India, a joint statement said on Saturday.

title: "Abandon Politics Of Confusion": BJP Slams Rahul Gandhi's NEET Leak Remark
content: BJP's Sanjay Mayukh accused Rahul Gandhi of spreading confusion by making baseless allegations to exploit the "tragic situation" to serve his own political agenda.

title: 'Centre Playing With Lives Of 22-23 Lakh Students': AAP On NEET Leak
content: AAP leader Anurag Dhanda questioned whether any effective measures have been taken to break down the syndicate behind the paper leak.

title: "He Wanted To Join BJP": NCP Slams Rohit Pawar Over Claims Of Rift In Party
content: Umesh Patil's remarks come in response to Rohit Pawar's claim on Wednesday that 22 NCP MLAs were in touch with the BJP and may switch sides soon.

title: Rs 30,000 For 3rd Child, Rs 40,000 For 4th: Chandrababu Naidu's Population Incentive
content: The Andhra Pradesh chief minister said rising incomes are leading to some couples giving birth to only one child.

title: Archaeological Body Allows Hindus Unrestricted Access To Bhojshala
content: A day after the Madhya Pradesh High Court ruled that Bhojshala in Dhar district was a temple of Goddess Saraswati, the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) on Saturday granted the Hindus an unrestricted access to the monument for worship and other purposes.

title: Midnight Demolition Drive Targeting Temples Sparks Protest In Rajkot
content: As municipal teams arrived at the location, locals gathered on the road to stage a peaceful demonstration, chanting religious hymns to protect the structure.

title: PM Modi Meets Dutch Royals In Netherlands, Discusses Tech, Trade
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday met King of the Netherlands Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima at the Royal Palace and discussed close cooperation in digital technology, innovation, fintech and blue economy.

title: Flights From Delhi May Get Cheaper As Tax On Aviation Fuel Slashed
content: The decision, approved by the Delhi Cabinet on Friday, is expected to reduce fuel costs for airlines operating out of the national capital.

title: Stay With Siddaramaiah Or Go With DK Shivakumar, Congress' Karnataka Conundrum
content: While Siddaramaiah has been pushing for a cabinet reshuffle and the filling up of vacant ministerial positions, Shivakumar continues to nurture ambitions of taking over as chief minister during the current term.

title: In Late Night Raid, Cops Seize 2,100 Fake Liquor Bottles In Dry Gujarat
content: During their raid, officials discovered two large tanks holding approximately 1,000 liters of ethanol, which was used as the base for the fake spirits.

title: AIMPLB says HC verdict in Bhojshala case to be challenged in Supreme Court
content: the Kamal Maula Mosque Committee will challenge the verdict in the supreme court . the ruling is in direct conflict with the spirit and constitutional mandate of the Places of Worship Act, 1991 . a spokesperson for the IMPLB said the ruling has been delivered in disregard of historical evidence, revenue records, colonial-era official documents, gazetteers .

title: Import curbs on silver bars: Govt changes status from 'free' to 'restricted'
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: Of roses and redlines: Trump faces thorny questions on Taiwan and trade
content: the promise of Chinese rose seeds for the white house rose garden from Xi Jinping highlighted the increasingly thorny relationship between the world’s two largest powers . despite assertions to the contrary, the US foreign-policy establishment is concluding that the summit exposed a shifting balance of power .

title: VD Satheesan pips KC Venugopal to Kerala CM post: Does it signal power shift within Congress?
content: KC Venugopal, Rahul Gandhi and VD Satheesan pick for Kerala . the BJP won with a thumping margin to end the 10-year rule of the left front . he managed to pip a party heavyweight to the top post .

title: Hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius: How climate change may be fuelling the spread
content: Representative image (AI-generated)  Oshin Bhatia is a budding journalist driven by an insatiable curiosity and hunger for knowledge. She loves diving deep into complex issues and expressing them through compelling narratives. Her interests span human-interest stories, the environment, and the intersections where people, places, and stories meet. Read More

title: NC government buckles after BJP protest, promises liquor ban in J&K
content: BJP leaders and workers outside the police station as they stage a protest over the demand to ban liquor, in Srinagar

title: ‘Stopped from wearing jeans’, woman shoots father in Chandigarh village
content: a 21-year-old woman allegedly shot her father using his licensed revolver in Khalchian village . the injured man was rushed to Guru Nanak Dev Hospital, where his condition is reported to be stable . police initially received information about a man admitted to hospital with a gunshot injury .

title: ‘Never seen anything like this’: 4 Indians arrested in Canada extortion-kidnapping
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: With no HC relief, Union minister Bandi Sanjay's son finally surrenders
content: bandi bageerath surrendered before the police on saturday night . he was later taken to Narsingi police station . the union minister of state for home bandi Sanjay's son has been booked under the protection of children from sexual offences .

title: Vodafone rides govt relief, posts Rs 52k crore surprise profit
content: on saturday, it reported a net profit of Rs 51,986 crore against a loss of Rs 7,168 crore in the corresponding period of 2025 . this was thanks to a one-time accounting gain from govt’s adjusted gross revenue relief . the company’s board has approved the issuance of up to 430 crore warrants, each convertible into an equity share .

title: Why the government is identifying 100 products to cut import dependence
content: The Committee further recommends strengthening of domestic manufacturing capabilities to reduce dependence on imports particularly in crude petroleum, gold and electronic components and also focus on capacity building measures and strengthening supply chain. Value addition initiative should be at priority to enhance competitiveness and improve overall trade balance with focus on real ‘Make in India’ and ‘Aatmanirbhar Bharat’ Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: ‘Do not hide anything from me’: Stalin orders review of DMK poll debacle
content: former chief minister M K Stalin announced the constitution of a 38-member committee to travel across all 234 constituencies . he directed committee members to document criticisms levelled against him by party functionaries . "your only duty is to come back and tell me exactly what the party cadre say, without personal likes or dislikes," Stalin said .

title: Pune teacher on NTA panel, ‘source of bio paper leak’, held
content: manisha Gurunath Mandhare is a biology teacher at modern college of arts, science and commerce . she was questioned in Delhi and subsequently arrested . her arrest comes a day after the CBI nabbed retired professor P V Kulkarni .
